title: HEK293 cell culture for co-immunoprecipitation experiments
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7dsd8","text":"Here, we describe HEK293 cell culture for the purpose of co-immunoprecipitation experiments","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"8cr7c","text":"Cells should be routinely tested for mycoplasma contamination using MycoAlert detection kit (Lonza, LT07)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Culture HEK293 cells in DMEM (Corning) supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum (HyClone)
Maintain cells at 37 degrees Celsius with 5% CO 2
In preparation for co-immunoprecipitation experiments, passage HEK cells with Trypsin onto three 10 cm tissue culture dishes per experimental condition, at a density of 1:10
24 hours later, transfect using FuGENE 6 (6-12 µg total plasmid DNA; Promega)
creator: Dan Dou
